project.intent: UWZ is a paintball-based fun and entertainment center where guests book life celebrations and events via online bookings.
project.orientation: Cargo workspace monorepo. Rust (Actix-web) API server, Svelte frontends (Tauri desktop + web), MySQL database. Rust SDK for Tauri surfaces, TypeScript SDK for the website.

principles.statement: All surfaces connect to the server exclusively through an SDK. No direct database connections or raw HTTP calls from surfaces.
principles.violation: A surface importing a DB driver, constructing raw HTTP requests, or bypassing the SDK to reach the server.

principles.statement: Tools enforce schema contracts. No schema changes without GUARDIAN review.
principles.violation: A tool or skill modifying project-schema.json or business-schema.json without running through the GUARDIAN approval flow.

principles.statement: All implementation plans must be red-teamed before execution.
principles.violation: A plan being implemented without first being challenged through a red team or review step.

structure.repo: Monorepo with server/ Cargo workspace and standalone api-contracts/ crate at root. Naming: server/, sdk-{lang}/, cli-{name}/, surface-{name}/. Build pipeline: cargo xtask build-all (api-contracts → schema-emitter → dist/openapi.json → server). Branches: worktrees per crate → dev → staging → production. Cross-cutting work on dev. Hotfixes on dev pushed through staging to main.

crates.server: Rust/Actix-web API server. Owns all data and business logic. Interacts with MySQL. No direct connections from surfaces.
crates.api-contracts: Standalone shared types crate at monorepo root. DTOs, OpenAPI path specs, and security modifiers. Zero server dependencies (serde, utoipa, chrono). Consumed by server workspace via path deps; SDKs will consume directly when real.
crates.sdk-rust: Rust SDK consumed by Tauri-based GUI surfaces (command-center, member). Wraps server API.
crates.sdk-ts: TypeScript SDK consumed by the website. Wraps server API.
crates.cli-idropr: Photo album upload and management CLI tool. Multi-part uploads to server.
crates.cli-api-testing: API endpoint testing CLI tool. Proof of correctness and agreement between server and database.
crates.surface-command-center: Tauri desktop GUI for staff at the physical location. Uses sdk-rust.
crates.surface-member: Tauri desktop member portal. Features TBD. Uses sdk-rust.
crates.surface-website: Public Svelte website. Online booking, NPS reviews, photo album viewing. Uses sdk-ts.

structure.mental_model: Server is the platform. Surfaces are consumers that reach the server only through SDKs. api-contracts is the shared contract boundary — all API types originate here and are consumed by server, SDKs, and surfaces. Rust SDK serves Tauri desktop apps (command-center, member). TypeScript SDK serves the website. CLI tools use the Rust SDK for operational tasks (photo uploads, secrets management).

surfaces.intent: Public-facing website where customers book events, submit NPS reviews, and view photo albums.
Staff-facing desktop application for on-site operations at the physical location.
Member portal for returning customers. Exact features TBD.

open_questions: Customer waivers: high-touch for staff in command-center, but should submission flow through website, member portal, or a dedicated surface?
Photos are first-class citizens. The website should deliver an incredible photo experience. How do we architect this?
How do we inject business/marketing/advertising/philosophy into the schema system?
Worktree workflow: worktrees persist and rebase onto dev after merge. Cross-cutting on dev. Hotfixes on dev → staging → main. Needs validation in practice.
New MySQL workflow: local MySQL pushing to cloud MySQL. Untested workflow — unclear how it will go.

resolved_questions.question: UX spec implementation: models struggle with current UX/UI spec format. How do we fix the tooling?
resolved_questions.resolution: Structured ViewModel specs (data + actions + states per route), type-enforced design system (TypeScript unions as the design vocabulary, Tailwind resolved only inside primitives), and screenshot feedback loop. Documented in UX.md. Three contracts designed but held pending per-crate schema architecture: type-enforced-design-system, two-tier-layout-model, component-layering.

resolved_questions.question: Build order starts clear (db → server → ts-SDK → rust-SDK → website) but full build sequence needs planning and red-teaming.
resolved_questions.resolution: Resolved by cargo xtask build-all. Build order: api-contracts → schema-emitter (emits dist/openapi.json) → server (includes dist/openapi.json via include_str!). SDK and surface steps are stubs pending migration. DEC-011 and DEC-012 document the key decisions.

per_crate_schemas.approach: Option C: Per-crate standalone schemas, no inheritance. Each crate gets its own schema file with contracts relevant to that crate. No merging, no inheritance chain, no discovery logic.
per_crate_schemas.rationale: Each surface has a different audience, design intent, and distribution model. A shared surface schema would over-constrain or lowest-common-denominator. Plugging everything into the global schema causes context bloat every session pays for. Drift between crate schemas is manageable via periodic cross-cutting monorepo analysis.

rejected_alternatives.scoped_contracts_in_global: One file with scope filters. Avoids inheritance but bloats the global schema — every session loads every crate's contracts.
rejected_alternatives.category_schemas: Shared schemas per category (surface, cli, platform). Fewer files but still needs inheritance design, and surfaces are too different for a shared schema.
rejected_alternatives.inherited_schemas: Per-crate schemas inheriting from global. Creates undefined merge semantics, discovery problems, and sync burden.

open_items: File naming convention: crate-schema.json? schema.json in crate root?
GUARDIAN needs to know which schema to load per context — branch name, crate dir, or explicit parameter?
How to handle cross-cutting contracts that genuinely apply to all crates (if any emerge beyond principles)?
Drift detection: periodic cross-cutting analysis workflow needs design.
